message:
fix(packs/core/filesystem): close backslash-escape bypass on rm -rf \/

Shell treats `\/` identically to `/` (backslash escapes the next
character) and `\~` identically to `~`. So these forms wipe the same
targets as the unquoted forms but previously slipped past the
rm-rf-root-home critical rule:

  rm -rf \/
  rm -rf \~
  rm -r -f \/
  rm --recursive --force \/

Added `['"\\]?` (allowing optional single-quote, double-quote, OR
backslash) before `[/~]` in all three Critical root/home variants.

Regression tests added for backslash-escaped forms.
